questions.question: Explain the meaning of population.
questions.answer: Population refers to the total number of people living in a particular area. It is an important factor in social and economic development.

questions.question: Explain the importance of population in a country.
questions.answer: Population is important because people develop resources, create technology and contribute to economic growth.

questions.question: Explain the concept of census.
questions.answer: Census is the official enumeration of population conducted periodically to collect demographic data.

questions.question: Explain India's population size according to the 2011 Census.
questions.answer: According to the 2011 Census, India's population was about 1.21 billion which is over 17% of the world's population.

questions.question: Explain population distribution in India.
questions.answer: Population distribution in India is uneven. Some regions like the Northern Plains are densely populated while mountainous areas are sparsely populated.

questions.question: Explain the concept of population density.
questions.answer: Population density refers to the number of persons living per square kilometre of land area.

questions.question: Explain why the Northern Plains have high population density.
questions.answer: The Northern Plains have fertile soil, flat land and good irrigation which support agriculture and settlements.

questions.question: Explain why mountainous regions have sparse population.
questions.answer: Mountainous regions have rugged terrain, harsh climate and poor accessibility which discourage settlement.

questions.question: Explain the concept of population growth.
questions.answer: Population growth refers to the increase in the number of people in a country over a period of time.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of absolute increase in population.
questions.answer: Absolute increase refers to the difference between the population of two time periods.

questions.question: Explain birth rate.
questions.answer: Birth rate is the number of live births per thousand persons in a year.

questions.question: Explain death rate.
questions.answer: Death rate is the number of deaths per thousand persons in a year.

questions.question: Explain the processes of population change.
questions.answer: Population change occurs due to three main processes: birth rate, death rate and migration.

questions.question: Explain migration.
questions.answer: Migration refers to the movement of people from one place to another for better opportunities.

questions.question: Explain internal migration.
questions.answer: Internal migration is the movement of people within a country from one region to another.

questions.question: Explain rural to urban migration.
questions.answer: Rural to urban migration occurs when people move from villages to cities in search of employment and better living conditions.

questions.question: Explain push factors of migration.
questions.answer: Push factors are conditions such as poverty and unemployment that force people to leave rural areas.

questions.question: Explain pull factors of migration.
questions.answer: Pull factors are attractions such as employment opportunities, education and healthcare facilities in cities.

questions.question: Explain urban population.
questions.answer: Urban population refers to the people living in towns and cities.

questions.question: Explain million-plus cities.
questions.answer: Million-plus cities are cities with a population of more than one million people.

questions.question: Explain adolescent population.
questions.answer: Adolescent population refers to people in the age group of 10–19 years.

questions.question: Explain the importance of adolescent population.
questions.answer: Adolescents form the future workforce and play an important role in the country's development.

questions.question: Explain the health challenges faced by adolescents.
questions.answer: Many adolescents suffer from poor nutrition and anaemia due to inadequate diet.

questions.question: Explain National Population Policy 2000.
questions.answer: National Population Policy 2000 aims to control population growth and improve family welfare through education and healthcare.

questions.question: Explain why population planning is necessary.
questions.answer: Population planning helps balance population growth with available resources and ensures sustainable development.
